obligation_text: The quality management system shall include: resource management, including human resources-related measures.
scoring_text: Knowledge of human resource management for AI quality management, including competency requirements, training programs, and resource allocation for AI system development and compliance.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of human resource management for AI quality management, including competency requirements, training programs, and resource allocation for AI system development and compliance.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of responsible AI principles and their operationalization, including fairness metrics, explainability methods, human oversight requirements, and AI impact assessment methodologies.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 17(1)(k) (competency) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-004 is the strongest match (STS: 0.616, cosine: 0.696). Secondary: AG-K-007 (AG, cosine: 0.686), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.633).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.616). Shared vocabulary: [ai, human, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act competency requirement terms [allocation, competency, compliance, development, management, programs] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, explainability, fairness, impact, methodologies, methods] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.895 (P=0.898, R=0.893). Balanced P/R — the 23-word EU AI Act competency and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (92.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.696 > STS 0.616 (gap +0.080). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, human, knowledge, requirements] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 23.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, human, knowledge, requirements]. EU AI Act-only: [allocation, competency, compliance, development, management, programs]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, explainability, fairness, impact, methodologies, methods].
